title: ARM SoC PMIC Platform Stack
body:
# ARM SoC PMIC Platform Stack

**Platform stack:** `arm_soc_template` · **Era:** 2007–2026 · **Vendor:** ARM ecosystem · **Count:** 42

Mobile SoC companion template — DDRC, PMIC rail sequencing, GIC interrupt, clock/reset, pin controller, USB PHY — paired with Apple/Snapdragon/Exynos/MediaTek host dies.

## Stack doctrine

This page lists every combinatorics leaf stamped `platform_stack: arm_soc_template` plus companion dies emitted from `field-cpu-companion-seed.json`. Host and guest CPUs matching the stack's `match` rules receive `always_with` linkage at combinatorics build — no manual wiring.

## Manufacturing notes — companion dies

- **ARM SoC DDRC Template** (ARM, 64-bit) — DDR/LPDDR memory controller — always-with host SoC
- **ARM SoC PMIC Template** (ARM, 32-bit) — Power Management IC — buck/LDO rail sequencing
- **ARM GIC Interrupt Ctrl** (ARM, 64-bit) — Generic Interrupt Controller — GICv3/v4 distributor
- **ARM SoC Clock/Reset** (ARM, 32-bit) — CRU/PLL — CPU/GPU/DDR clock tree template
- **ARM SoC Pin Controller** (ARM, 32-bit) — Pin mux + pad config — mobile SoC GPIO template
- **ARM SoC USB PHY** (ARM, 32-bit) — USB3/Type-C PHY companion — PMIC-rail coupled

## Catalog ids

The `chip_ids` array holds all **42** entries for this platform stack, sorted alphabetically. Run `field-ironclad-chips-combinatorics.py combinatronic` for path percents and imprint renders.

*Stack: arm_soc_template · seed: data/field-cpu-companion-seed.json · citation: ironclad:chips:3*
